body: Throughout the ages, borders have shifted, empires have risen and fallen, and names have appeared and faded from the map. Europa Historica invites you to explore these changes, to follow territories, trace rulers, and watch how history unfolds across space and time.

This project is built step by step by a single developer and supported by artificial intelligence, but it stands on the shoulders of open knowledge. Much of what you see here is made possible by the work of:
body2: This project would not be possible without open knowledge and the people who make it accessible.
If you enjoy exploring, notice something, or have ideas to improve the map, feedback is always welcome — reach out on Instagram.
Thank you and stay curious, there is always something new to discover!

about.title: About this project
paragraphs: Europa Historica combines historical map data with structured knowledge to make political change visible over time.

The territorial shapes are based on datasets from Historical Basemaps. These are combined with information from Wikipedia and Wikidata to connect places, states, and rulers across different periods. Ancient settlements are sourced from the Pleiades gazetteer.
list: borders are approximations based on available historical data
names and contexts can change depending on time and interpretation
the project evolves continuously as new data is added and refined
paragraphs2: The map currently covers over 295 territories from 8000 BC to 1500 AD, with rulers, flags, and historical context for many of them. It includes an interactive tutorial, narrative trails, a historical events layer, ancient settlements, and a global search — and works on mobile as well. The interface and all content are available in English and German; switch any time via the language toggle in the bottom right.
All content is bundled with the app — descriptions, images, and event data ship locally. The map keeps working without a constant connection to Wikipedia or Wikidata.
Europa Historica is built step by step as an independent project, exploring how history can be experienced interactively and not just read.

It was created to address a simple problem: history we read rarely fits into context as clearly as history we see. Too often, history is understood in isolation, rather than within the world and time in which it existed.
If you spot any mistakes or have ideas, feel free to reach out on Instagram.

updates.title: Updates
updates.intro: Europa Historica is in active development. The map currently covers 8000 BC to 2010 AD with over 295 territories and 3,000+ rulers. All epochs from prehistory through 1500 AD are now fully populated with territories, descriptions, and ruler lists where available.
recently: New trail: From Mecca to Vienna — 622 to 1683, from the Hijra to the relief of the Vienna siege, in ten steps tracing the long encounter between Islam and Europe.
New trail: Road to Germany — 1806 to 1990, from the end of the Holy Roman Empire to reunification, in nine steps.
City tooltips with Wikipedia intros — Pleiades settlements now show longer descriptions in EN and DE, with a disclaimer when the article is about the modern town at the same site.
German language version — the entire interface and all content (tooltips, tutorial, trails, events, search) is now fully available in German. Language toggle in the bottom right.
Fully offline-capable — all descriptions, ruler images, and event content are now bundled with the app. No live calls to Wikipedia at runtime.
1100–1500 AD complete — Mamluk Sultanate, Ilkhanate, Teutonic Knights, Lithuania, Timurid Empire, Marinid dynasty, Sultanate of Rum, and more
Almohad Caliphate, Almoravid dynasty, Khwarezm-Shah, Hafsid Caliphate, Empire of Trebizond — full ruler lists
500–1000 AD batch: ~40 territories (Balts, Rus' Khaganate, Icelandic Commonwealth, and more)
Historical events layer — 39 key events across all eras, with descriptions and links
Two narrative trails — Byzantine Empire and the history of writing
Multi-context territories: Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Serbia, Croatia, Georgia, France, Germany, Austria, Prussia, Persia
coming: Grand Duchy of Moscow → Tsardom → Empire — multi-context territory
Battles as a separate event type
updates.feedback: Feedback and corrections are always welcome on Instagram.
